title: plagiobothrys fulvus var. campestris
intro: Plagiobothrys fulvus var. campestris, also known as Campestris Wild Heliotrope, is a flowering plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Plagiobothrys fulvus var. campestris include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as its ability to support digestive health. The plant can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, and it is also sometimes used in herbal teas, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
